items.briefing:
Deepfake fraud attacks have surged 180% globally, with identity verification systems increasingly fooled by AI-generated fake videos and voices. This is a growing risk for Australian organisations that rely on digital identity checks for onboarding, finance approvals, or remote access. Separately, cybercriminals are now hiding invisible text inside emails to trick AI-powered spam and security filters, meaning malicious emails may slip through even modern defences.

Experts are also warning that AI tools — including automated agents used in business workflows — need stronger human oversight to prevent unintended or harmful actions. Australia's skills shortage in this area makes that harder.

**Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected request via video call or voice message to transfer funds or share access credentials, always verify through a separate, known contact method before acting.

featured.title: DigiCert survey finds 81% of Australian organisations hit by AI security incidents or vulnerabilities
featured.quote: 81% of Australian organisations already experiencing AI security incidents shows threat is immediate and widespread.
